what_it_does: Acceptance test that pins the node-state registry behavior: compute nodes keep idle/ran/cached/stuck semantics, reference/portal nodes report live or empty, and the portal idle↔cached flip across run vs reload is removed.
observations: Uses a temporary FsStore and discovers nodes from company/nodes.
Defines a global PASS counter and a custom check() helper for assertions.
Tests that capabilities().node_states exposes six statuses with means, applies_to, and label fields.
Verifies applies_to scoping: idle/ran/cached/stuck apply to resolve-mode compute; live/empty apply to reference.
Exercises Suite.create_node, delete_node, run, and state on constant, uppercase, and portal node types.
Checks stable live status for a resolving portal across fresh result and two reloads.
Uses run:// URI refs to configure portal node references.
standouts: Framed as a two-step proof: Step A preserves compute-node statuses unchanged; Step B additively introduces live/empty for portals.
Directly asserts the removal of the run-vs-reload idle↔cached flip for portals.
Treats the node_states registry exposed by capabilities() as the single source of UI truth.
conventions: Top-level script with try/finally cleanup of a temporary store directory.
Global PASS counter and custom check() for test reporting instead of a test framework.
Large module docstring used as an executable design/acceptance specification.
Hardcoded node type names and graph names for direct scenario construction.
Uses tempfile and shutil for an isolated test store.
concerns: Relies on global state and sys.path manipulation at module top.
Hardcodes node-type behavior assumptions without visible fixtures, making it brittle if node definitions change.
Test section numbering in comments is out of order (section 4 appears before sections 1-3).
No explicit teardown of suite/store beyond shutil.rmtree.
notes: Acceptance test that locks in a registered node-state vocabulary and the new live/empty semantics for reference portals while preserving existing compute-node statuses.
questions: What is the exact contract and implementation of Suite.capabilities() and Suite.state(result=...)?
Where is Suite.NODE_STATES defined and how is the registry populated?
What determines whether a node uses resolve-mode compute or reference?
How does the scheduler persist and recall stuck status across reload?
What should happen when a portal ref that was live becomes dangling?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it proves a registered single-source node-state set, preserves compute statuses, adds live/empty for portals, and removes the reload flip, and the code exercises exactly those claims.
apparent_themes: Cross-path stability between fresh result and reload
Acceptance testing as executable design specification
intention_signals: Codify a design shift from hardcoded status enums to a declared, capability-exposed node-state registry, while guaranteeing that reference-resolved portals get their own stable status vocabulary and never masquerade as idle/cached compute nodes.
novelty: Introduces live and empty as first-class registered statuses for reference-resolved portals and explicitly tests the elimination of the run-vs-reload idle↔cached flip.
contribution: Provides an executable acceptance contract for the node-state registry: it keeps the four compute statuses intact, adds live/empty for portals, and asserts stable portal status across run and reload paths. A fused system would keep this as a regression guard for state-type semantics and registry exposure.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for a node-state registry that verifies compute nodes retain idle/ran/cached/stuck semantics, reference portals report live or empty, and portal status is stable across run and reload.
intention_for_embedding: To lock in the registered node-state vocabulary and prove that adding portal-specific statuses does not change existing compute-node behavior or reintroduce the idle/cached reload flip.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: Custom lightweight test assertion used throughout the acceptance script; on failure it raises with a labeled message, otherwise it tallies a passing check and prints a status line.

symbols.does: Extracts the status field of a node from a suite state dictionary by node id.
symbols.description: Small lookup helper that finds the node entry matching nid in st['nodes'] and returns its 'status' value, centralizing how the test reads node states.
